sessions.intent: Architect Piece 1: Identity Module for runtime2 builder v2

before.assumptions: Runtime2 has IDataSource, MemoryStack with lazy resolvers, module pattern with [Action] handlers. No identity module exists yet.
before.risk: Low — identity is standalone, no dependencies on other new pieces.

sessions.plan: Identity module with 6 actions (create, get, getAll, archive, setDefault, export). IdentityVariable type with [Sensitive] on PrivateKey, ToString()=PublicKey. Storage via System.DataSource table 'identity'. Lazy %MyIdentity% resolver on System MemoryStack. Per-actor variables: %MyIdentity% (System), %Identity% (User), %ServiceIdentity% (Service). New [Sensitive] attribute + SensitivePropertyFilter for two-mode serialization. Ed25519 key generation via NSec, internal to module.

actions.context: Full architecture plan for identity module, designed interactively with Ingi

after.health: Plan approved, branch created, ready for test-designer
after.notes: Key design decisions: per-actor identity (not shared), [Sensitive] attribute for two-mode serialization, System.DataSource storage, lazy auto-create on %MyIdentity% access

sessions.intent: Create test stubs for Identity module (handlers, IdentityVariable, MyIdentity resolver, [Sensitive] attribute, PLang integration)

before.assumptions: Identity module follows settings module pattern — DataSource storage, MemoryStack resolver, handler CRUD actions. [Sensitive] is new infrastructure.
before.risk: Low — test stubs only, no production code

sessions.plan: Write 40 test stubs across 4 C# test files and 8 PLang test goal files covering identity CRUD, variable type, lazy resolver, sensitive attribute filtering, and full-pipeline PLang integration.

actions.context: C# test stubs following SettingsDataTests pattern — engine setup/teardown, Assert.Fail bodies
PLang integration test stubs — Start goal, throw 'not implemented' bodies

after.health: 48 test stubs created (40 C# + 8 PLang). Two open questions flagged for architect.
after.notes: Questions: (1) duplicate name when archived — error or re-create? (2) PrivateKey dot navigation — blocked or serialization-only?

sessions.intent: Resolve test-designer open questions, add rename + unarchive actions

before.assumptions: Test-designer flagged two design questions. Ingi will confirm direction.
before.risk: Low — design decisions only, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Resolve both questions: (1) duplicate name = always error, add unarchive + rename actions. (2) [Sensitive] is serialization only, dot navigation works. Update plan to v2 with new actions and corrected test expectations.

actions.context: Summary of test-designer questions and architect decisions
Updated plan with 8 actions (was 6), 59 tests (was 48), resolved open questions

after.health: v2 plan ready. Two new actions (unarchive, rename), one test renamed, 11 new tests specified. Ready for test-designer to update stubs, then coder.
after.notes: Ingi confirmed both decisions. Names are identities — never reuse across archived. [Sensitive] is serialization boundary, not access control.

sessions.intent: Implement identity module: 8 actions, [Sensitive] infrastructure, MyIdentity resolver, 49 C# tests

before.assumptions: Architect v2 plan is final. Test stubs exist. NSec is already referenced. Settings module provides the DataSource pattern to follow.
before.risk: Medium — new module with new infrastructure ([Sensitive]), but follows established patterns.

sessions.plan: Phase 1: [Sensitive] infrastructure (SensitiveAttribute, SensitivePropertyFilter, wire into JsonStreamSerializer). Phase 2: Identity types (IdentityVariable with persistence, KeyGenerator with Ed25519). Phase 3: IdentityData on Actor + %MyIdentity% DynamicData registration. Phase 4: 8 identity handlers (create, get, getAll, archive, unarchive, rename, setDefault, export). Phase 5: Implement 49 C# test bodies. Phase 6: PLang test goals (mark pending — need builder prompt update).

actions.context: Phase 1: [Sensitive] attribute + filter infrastructure
Phase 2-3: Identity types with OBP persistence, Ed25519 key generation, lazy IdentityData resolver
Phase 3: Add lazy Identity property + register %MyIdentity% DynamicData on MemoryStack
Phase 4: 8 identity CRUD handlers following settings module pattern
Phase 5: Implemented all 51 C# test bodies — all pass
Fixed pre-existing namespace collision: global using Serializers shadowed by using directive

after.health: All production code implemented, all 51 C# tests pass (1645/1645 total suite). PLang tests pending builder prompt update.
after.notes: Also fixed pre-existing SerializerRegistryTests.cs namespace collision. Identity module follows settings module pattern exactly.

sessions.intent: Analyze identity module code for OBP compliance, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning, and deletion tests

before.assumptions: Coder implemented 8 handlers, IdentityVariable, IdentityData, KeyGenerator, SensitivePropertyFilter, and Actor.Identity. 51 C# tests pass.
before.risk: Low — analysis only, no code changes.

sessions.plan: 5-pass analysis: (1) OBP compliance, (2) Simplification, (3) Readability, (4) Behavioral reasoning, (5) Deletion tests. Focus on identity module files + infrastructure changes.

actions.context: 5-pass analysis of 15 files. Found 1 behavioral bug (get.cs overwrites MyIdentity on by-name fetch), duplicate auto-create, non-atomic rename, 2 test gaps, 2 minor cleanups.

after.health: Analysis complete. Verdict: FAIL. 1 bug, 4 needs-work items, 2 test gaps.
after.notes: Critical: get.cs line 24 calls Identity.Update() on by-name fetch, corrupting %MyIdentity%. Recommend sending back to coder.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of identity module implementation (coder v1)

before.assumptions: Coder reports 51 C# tests pass, 1645 total suite. PLang tests are stubs. Need to verify test honesty and coverage.
before.risk: Low — read-only analysis, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Run full test suite, extract coverage, analyze test quality (weak assertions, false greens, missing edge cases, deletion test), check PLang test status, write test-report.json with findings and verdict.

actions.context: Test quality report: 8 findings (4 major, 4 minor), verdict needs-fixes
Cobertura XML coverage output from TUnit --coverage

after.health: Analysis complete. Verdict: needs-fixes. 3 major findings require coder action.
after.notes: Key gaps: export default path untested (60% line), two weak error key assertions, all PLang tests are stubs.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder v2 fixes for identity module

before.assumptions: Coder v2 addressed all 7 findings from analyzer v1. Need to verify fixes are correct and check for regressions.
before.risk: Low — focused re-review of fix-introduced code only.

sessions.plan: Verify each of 7 fixes: trace by-name path, check GetOrCreateDefaultAsync edge cases, verify rename atomicity, validate new tests.

actions.context: Re-review of 4 changed files. All 7 original fixes correct. 1 new finding: GetOrCreateDefaultAsync doesn't check SaveAsync result.

after.health: Analysis complete. Verdict: FAIL (soft). 1 medium-severity regression in consolidated method.
after.notes: GetOrCreateDefaultAsync line 88 doesn't check SaveAsync result. Original Get.Run() did check it. Quick fix.

sessions.intent: Fix GetOrCreateDefaultAsync SaveAsync result check (code analyzer v2 finding)

before.assumptions: Single medium-severity finding: SaveAsync result not checked in GetOrCreateDefaultAsync. Quick fix.
before.risk: Low — small change, throw on save failure.

sessions.plan: Add SaveAsync result check in types.cs (throw InvalidOperationException on failure). Add try/catch in get.cs to convert to Data.FromError. IdentityData.cs unchanged — throw propagates naturally.

actions.context: Check SaveAsync result, throw on failure to prevent phantom identity
Catch InvalidOperationException from GetOrCreateDefaultAsync, return Data.FromError

after.notes: Throw on save failure restores the error check that existed in v1. Both callers handle it correctly.

sessions.intent: Final verification of SaveAsync check fix

before.assumptions: Coder v3 added SaveAsync result check and try/catch in get.cs. Should be a quick verification.

sessions.plan: Verify throw in types.cs, catch in get.cs, propagation through IdentityData.ResolveDefault().

actions.context: Final verification. All correct. No remaining findings.

after.health: PASS. Identity module is clean after 3 rounds of review.
after.notes: All original findings resolved. Module ready for merge.

sessions.intent: Re-review test quality after coder v2/v3 fixes

before.assumptions: Coder v2 fixed 7 codeanalyzer findings, v3 added SaveAsync check. Need to verify fixes and check for regressions.

sessions.plan: Re-run tests and coverage. Check v1 findings status. Look for new issues from refactoring.

actions.context: Updated report: 4 findings (1 major flaky test, 3 minor carried over), verdict needs-fixes
Updated coverage data showing improvements

after.health: 1 flaky test found, 2 minor weak assertions remain, PLang stubs still pending.
after.notes: Sensitive_IdentityVariable_PrivateKeyExcluded fails intermittently due to base64 '+' escaping. Only blocking issue.

sessions.intent: Fix flaky test and weak assertions from tester v2

before.assumptions: 3 actionable findings: 1 flaky test (base64 escaping), 2 weak assertions.

sessions.plan: Fix Sensitive_IdentityVariable_PrivateKeyExcluded by deserializing JSON and comparing values. Add Error.Key assertions for whitespace create and missing setDefault.

actions.context: Replace raw Contains() with JsonElement property comparison to avoid base64 escaping flakiness
Add Error.Key assertions for whitespace create and missing setDefault cases

after.health: All 1647 tests pass. No flaky failures.
after.notes: PLang test stubs remain (blocked on builder prompt) — tracked separately.

sessions.intent: Deep analysis after coder v4 — look for missed issues

before.assumptions: All previous findings fixed. Need to look harder at cross-handler interactions.

sessions.plan: Verify v4 fixes. Apply deletion test to every code path. Trace cross-handler interactions for edge cases.

actions.context: Found auto-create overwrite bug — data loss when 'default' name is already taken

after.health: 1 major new finding: GetOrCreateDefaultAsync overwrites existing 'default' identity.
after.notes: Data loss bug. Missed in v1/v2 because tests never combine Create(default, SetAsDefault=false) with auto-create.

sessions.intent: Fix auto-create overwrite data loss bug (tester v3)

before.assumptions: GetOrCreateDefaultAsync overwrites existing 'default' identity. Fix: promote existing non-archived identity instead.
before.risk: Medium — changes auto-create behavior, need to update existing test expectations.

sessions.plan: Add promotion step in GetOrCreateDefaultAsync between find-default and auto-create. Update test that expected auto-create to expect promotion. Add 2 new tests.

actions.context: Add promotion of existing non-archived identity before auto-create
Update auto-create test, add overwrite prevention test, add export no-default test

after.health: All 1649 tests pass. Data loss bug fixed.
after.notes: 3-step resolution: find default → promote existing → auto-create. Only creates new identity when none exist.

sessions.intent: Final verification of auto-create overwrite fix

before.assumptions: Coder v5 fixed auto-create with 3-step promotion. Need to verify fix and new tests.

sessions.plan: Run tests, verify promotion logic, check new tests are honest (deletion test), issue verdict.

actions.context: Final report: verdict approved, all findings resolved

after.health: APPROVED. All 1649 tests pass. All findings from v1-v3 resolved.
after.notes: Only remaining gap: PLang test stubs (blocked on builder prompt). Recommend security analyst next.

sessions.intent: Security audit of identity module: blue team + red team analysis

before.assumptions: Identity module is new code with Ed25519 keys, [Sensitive] filtering, and SQLite persistence. Other bots (coder, analyzer, tester) have all signed off. No prior security review exists.
before.risk: Private key handling, key generation entropy, sensitive data leakage, race conditions in default identity resolution

sessions.plan: Blue team + red team security audit of identity module. Map attack surface across key generation, storage, serialization, name validation, default resolution, export handler. Write security-report.json, verdict, and result.md.

actions.context: Full security audit of identity module and supporting infrastructure. Traced all serialization paths, SQL query patterns, and key material lifecycle.
Security audit deliverables: structured report, detailed findings, verdict (PASS), summaries.

after.notes: 1 medium (theoretical Compress gap), 3 low accepted-risk. Identity module is well-designed for user-sovereign threat model.

sessions.intent: Cross-cutting integrity review of identity module after all three reviewers passed

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v3, tester v4, and security v1 all passed. 1649 tests pass. Need to check cross-file contracts and gaps between reviewers.
before.risk: Low — review only, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Read all code, all prior reports. Run tests. Focus on: cross-file error propagation, architectural fit, review gaps, foundation ripple. Write auditor-report.json and verdict.

actions.context: Cross-cutting review of all identity module files and supporting infrastructure. Found unhandled exception path in IdentityData.ResolveDefault() that codeanalyzer missed.
Auditor deliverables: structured report with 5 findings (1 major, 3 minor, 1 nit), verdict FAIL.

after.notes: Major: IdentityData.ResolveDefault() has no try/catch around GetOrCreateDefaultAsync which throws on save failure. Lazy %MyIdentity% access would crash. Recommend sending back to coder.

sessions.intent: Fix auditor v1 findings: unhandled exception, export consistency, envelope filter

before.assumptions: 3 fixes needed: try/catch in ResolveDefault, Export uses GetOrCreateDefaultAsync, SensitivePropertyFilter in Envelope.
before.risk: Low — targeted fixes with clear scope.

sessions.plan: 1. IdentityData.cs: try/catch InvalidOperationException in ResolveDefault, return null. 2. export.cs: use GetOrCreateDefaultAsync for null-name. 3. Data.Envelope.cs: add SensitivePropertyFilter to options. 4. Update export test.

actions.context: Add try/catch for unhandled exception in lazy resolver
Use GetOrCreateDefaultAsync for null-name path, consistent with Get
Add SensitivePropertyFilter to envelope JSON options (defense-in-depth)
Update export test to expect auto-create instead of NotFound

after.health: All 1649 tests pass. Auditor findings #1, #2, #3 fixed. #4, #5 skipped (known limitation, moot).
after.notes: Identity module ready for final review.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v6 fixes for auditor v1 findings

before.assumptions: Coder v6 addressed findings #1, #2, #3. Need to verify correctness and decide if tester re-review needed.

sessions.plan: Read all 4 changed files, run tests, verify each fix is correct and minimal. Assess whether tester needs to re-review.

actions.context: All 3 fixes correct and minimal. No regressions. 1649 tests pass.
Auditor v2 deliverables: PASS verdict, no tester re-review needed.

after.notes: No tester re-review needed. Suggest docs bot next.

sessions.intent: Documentation pass for identity module — final gate before merge

before.assumptions: Coder v6 complete, auditor PASS, tester APPROVED, security PASS. XML docs already in code. Architecture docs not yet updated.
before.risk: Low — documentation only, no code changes

sessions.plan: Update modules.md (add identity to handlers table + details section), good_to_know.md (3 new patterns: [Sensitive], IdentityData lazy resolution, %MyIdentity% DynamicData), README.md (object graph, file tree). Write CHANGELOG in result.md. Write reports and verdict.

actions.context: Added identity module to built-in handlers table and full details section
Added 3 cross-cutting pattern sections for identity-related infrastructure
Updated object graph, file tree, and View.cs description
CHANGELOG, docs report, and verdict

after.health: All documentation gaps filled. Verdict: PASS.
after.notes: PLang .goal examples flagged for tester (blocked on builder prompt). Not a merge blocker.
